mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) near zero percent reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, providing a structural floor for S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over the 20-day horizon in the post-2008 sample.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 9.5% signals persistent labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates healthy demand absorption without inventory buildup, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and RSAFS growth rates has a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression of forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) in a low-growth regime (GDPC1 < 14000) signals stagflationary pressure, triggering fears of policy tightening and compressing equity multiples.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL growth is associated with positive forward returns when GDPC1 is below 14000.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 600,000 units signal a recovery in the housing sector, which acts as a leading indicator for broader economic activity and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in > 60% of months where HOUST exceeds 600.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) provides immediate positive sentiment regarding labor market health, driving short-term positive equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) for 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) confirms a broad-based economic recovery, reducing recession risk premiums and supporting higher equity valuations.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDPC1 and falling UNRATE is associated with negative forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 0.25% indicates an aggressive easing stance that maximizes liquidity and supports risk appetite, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.
